books.title: Hooked
books.author: Asako Yuzuki
books.ai_comment: A tense novel of obsession and female friendship, where loneliness, status, and performance slowly curdle into fixation.

books.title: The Impossible City
books.author: Karen Cheung
books.comment: Parts were fantastic and made me feel like I was living real life in Hong Kong. Some sections were too specialized in the author's arts scene, but the pressure of daily life and choices really made me think.
books.ai_comment: A raw, affectionate map of Hong Kong that balances grief, humor, and the weight of belonging.

books.title: The Book of Tokyo
books.author: M.Emnrich et al
books.ai_comment: Snapshots of Tokyo that feel like overheard conversations and fleeting neon reflections.

books.title: Strange Houses
books.author: Uketsu
books.comment: Easy-to-read crime novel that hooks you quickly and keeps the story moving. I liked it a lot.
books.ai_comment: Short, uncanny folktales where the walls are listening and the exits keep moving.

books.title: XI - A Study in Power
books.author: Kerry Brown
books.comment: A very detailed look at Xi's politics. Interesting and one more step in my journey to understand the China to come.
books.ai_comment: Coolly argued biography that reads like a blueprint for consolidated power.

books.title: Cold Enough for Snow

books.ai_comment: A quiet river of a novella that asks how memory edits love and duty.

books.title: Heaven

books.ai_comment: Bleak and tender; examines cruelty at school without easy absolution.

books.title: Mild Vertigo

books.title: Piranesi

books.title: Scattered All Over the World
books.author: Yoko Tawada

books.title: We Do Not Part
books.author: Han Kang
books.comment: This felt focused on inner emotions and relationships more than physical context. I like Han Kang, but sometimes I struggled to follow every thread and what it all meant.

books.title: Vanishing World
books.author: Sayaka Murata
books.comment: This book hangs on its core idea. It is far-fetched but still connected enough to today's society to keep me very interested, especially in how human relationships may evolve.
books.ai_comment: Breezy apocalypse vignettes that treat oddity as common sense.

books.title: In Praise of Shadows
books.author: Junichiro Tanizaki
books.comment: Fantastic. Some parts are outdated, but most ideas still hold. It made me think about not only light, but also what light takes away and how the unseen can be beautiful.
books.ai_comment: A meditation on darkness and lacquerware; still feels current about tech and taste.

books.title: Earthlings
books.author: Sayaka Murata
books.ai_comment: Deadpan alienation turning into something feral and unforgettable.
